pattern: You may overwrite exactly two elements with anything you like, so the achievable minimum low score is always zero (duplicate the two overwritten values); the only real decision is which two elements to sacrifice to shrink the spread of what remains.
hints: You can set the two changed elements to any values at all, including making them equal to each other or to something already in the array.

Because the two changed values can always be made equal, the minimum possible low score is zero; the whole problem reduces to picking which two elements to remove so that the range of the rest is as small as possible.
Sort the array. The best removal is always two elements from the ends: both smallest, both largest, or one from each end. Compare those three candidates and take the smallest resulting range.

edge_inputs.description: already tightly clustered after one swap
extreme spread at minimum length collapses to zero
removing the two extremes is not optimal here
many duplicates with one outlier on each side

stress.description: uniformly random values at moderate size
clustered values with a few far outliers
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100000))
    base = rng.randint(1, 1000000000)
    vals = [max(1, min(1000000000, base + rng.randint(-5, 5))) for _ in range(n)]
    for _ in range(min(3, n)):
        idx = rng.randrange(n)
        vals[idx] = rng.randint(1, 1000000000)
    return [vals]
